project.name: Clawvato
project.description: Business operating system for solo technical founders — memory-powered AI agent across Slack, email, calendar, Drive, meetings, and Claude Code

E.name: Proactive Intelligence
E.notes: Sweep pipeline working end-to-end (130 facts stored). Sonnet extraction fixed. Context planner superseded by CC-native.

F.name: Security Hardening
F.notes: Training wheels removed. Pre-tool security checks + output sanitization remain.

G.name: GitHub + Self-Development
G.notes: Redesigned as self-development system. Three-gate safety model. Design at docs/DESIGN_TRACK_G_SELF_DEV.md.

H.name: Plugin Adapter + CC Bridge
H.notes: HTTP memory plugin deployed on Railway. All CC instances wired (local via public URL, Railway via internal URL). In-tree MCP server is now dead code — removal pending.

J.name: Hybrid Architecture
J.notes: Replaced by CC-Native Engine (Track O). Hybrid code preserved on main branch as fallback.

M.name: Background Sweeps
M.notes: Pipeline working end-to-end. 130 facts + 693 entities stored from first backfill.

N.name: Context Planner
N.notes: Superseded by CC-Native Engine — CC plans its own context natively.

O.name: CC-Native Engine
O.notes: Claude Code as core engine via Channels. Deployed on Railway. Now uses HTTP memory plugin (separate Railway service) instead of in-tree MCP server. Fireflies switched to native MCP tools for interactive use.

infrastructure.local: macOS, .mcp.json points to Railway HTTP memory plugin
infrastructure.dataVolume: /data (Claude CLI auth + gws config + debug workspaces)
infrastructure.deployMethod: GitHub auto-deploy — clawvato from cc-native-engine branch, clawvato-memory from main branch
infrastructure.maxPlan: Claude Max subscription — CC-native engine costs $0
infrastructure.memoryPlugin: github.com/andrewcovato/clawvato-memory — deployed as HTTP MCP server on Railway. Public: clawvato-memory-production.up.railway.app/mcp. Internal: clawvato-memory.railway.internal:8080/mcp
infrastructure.memoryPluginAuth: Bearer token via MCP_AUTH_TOKEN env var on both Railway services

metrics.pluginTestsPassing: 10/10 CRUD cycle + HTTP transport verified (health, initialize, tools/list, search_memory)

currentSprint.name: CC-Native Engine + Shared Memory Brain
goals: CC-Native Engine via Channels ✅ (deployed on Railway, working)
Eyes (👀) reaction lifecycle ✅ (add on receipt, remove on CC pickup)
Idle timeout with verified handoff protocol ✅
HTTP transport for memory plugin ✅ (deployed on Railway, all instances wired)
Local CC wired to HTTP memory ✅
Railway CC wired to HTTP memory (internal URL) ✅
Native Fireflies MCP for interactive queries ✅
currentSprint.progress: ~95% complete. HTTP memory plugin deployed and wired. In-tree cleanup and stability monitoring remaining.

lastSession.type: Native plugin audit + HTTP memory deployment + integration wiring + bug fixes
keyDecisions: Keep custom Gmail (native can't send), Calendar (less overhead), Slack (native can't react), Drive (no native exists)
Split Fireflies: native MCP for interactive queries, custom client for sweep ingestion
HTTP memory plugin deployed as separate Railway service — single source of truth for memory tools
MCP config type must be 'http' not 'url' — CC schema validation
No artificial caps on search results — sensible defaults but no hard maximums
Eyes reaction lifecycle: add on receipt, remove on CC pickup (code-enforced both ways)

immediateBacklog.title: Remove in-tree MCP server — dead code now that all instances use HTTP plugin. Delete src/mcp/memory/server.ts, src/mcp/memory/stdio.ts, .cc-native-mcp.json.
Implement per-session memory scoping — BLOCKING further memory usage scaling. Owner directive: no dev memories until this is built.
Monitor cc-native engine for 24h with HTTP memory wiring. Verify session restart + handoff works with remote MCP.
Add LLM reranking to plugin retrieve_context — tsvector-only retrieval will degrade at 1K+ memories.

backlog.title: Track G Phase 1: read-only GitHub access
Track G Phase 2: branch + PR creation (self-development, owner-gated)
Tiered token budgets for retrieval — high-importance facts should get more budget
Hierarchical memory summarization — critical at 50K+ memories
Upgrade embedding model from all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384-dim) — evaluate when memory count exceeds 10K
Session topology for cloud instance — design when Slack volume exceeds ~50 msgs/day across 5+ topics
Serve SKILL.md as MCP resource from plugin server
SessionEnd hook for memory extraction safety net
Track G Phase 3: self-deploy with health check + auto-rollback

thingsNotToRedo: DON'T REBUILD WHAT COMES NATIVELY WITH CLAUDE CODE
Don't hardcode prompts in TypeScript — all prompts in config/prompts/*.md
Don't hardcode tunables in TypeScript — all operational values in config/default.json
Always take the pain now — build for durability, no short-term hacks
Always build for the future — adopt Anthropic tech as it ships
Bug reports → investigate + plan + confirm before coding
Plan before coding — don't jump straight to implementation
Don't build bespoke tools/tables for specific entity types — unified memory handles it
Store what+why, never how — implementation details don't belong in memory
MCP stdio servers MUST redirect ALL logging to stderr
Plugin schema must exactly match Railway DB — test against real DB, not assumptions
Always use soft-delete (valid_until = NOW()) for memories, never hard DELETE
Hard DELETE can dangle superseded_by FK references
CC instances won't know about MCP tools unless system prompt teaches them
Global ~/.claude/.mcp.json may not load for a specific project — use project-scoped config
Don't store clawvato dev memories to the shared brain until project scoping is implemented
Memory retrieval quality degrades at scale — scoping is the #1 lever, then reranking, then summarization
Haiku max output 8192 tokens — use Sonnet for extraction
Zod schema defaults override config/default.json — must update BOTH
MCP config type must be 'http' not 'url' — CC schema validation rejects 'url'
Expect heredocs (<< 'EOF') prevent bash interpolation — use export + $env(VAR) for Tcl
MCP SDK Server.connect() is 1:1 — for HTTP concurrency, create new Server+Transport per request
Railway CLI source.repo doesn't trigger GitHub webhook — connect repo through dashboard
Don't impose artificial caps on query results — sensible defaults, no hard maximums
